profile.name: David
profile.role_in_household: Primary client and higher earner; tends to steer the agenda and push for concrete action items.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High: comfortable with investing/taxes and tracking multiple income streams; less interested in day-to-day spending minutiae.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but downside-aware; will take market risk if the plan is structured, diversified, and the worst-case is discussed first.
profile.communication_style: Direct, quick, and numbers-forward; corrects small inaccuracies; uses qualifiers like "roughly" or "I might be off" when recalling expenses; asks pointed follow-ups.
emotional_tendencies: health-related worry that spikes around planning topics
relief when there is a checklist and timeline
profile.decision_making_style: Research-and-compare; wants options with tradeoffs (tax impact, liquidity, risk) and then decides decisively.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; expects transparency on assumptions, fees, and what the advisor does vs what the clients must do.
internal_biases: overweighting income stability based on recent patterns
hidden_concerns: whether disability coverage truly matches needs and definitions
fear of missing tax optimization opportunities
worry that accounts may be inefficiently organized despite strong totals

profile.name: Jennifer
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on lifestyle, cash flow comfort, and keeping plans simple enough to maintain.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands basics and asks good practical questions, but occasionally mixes terms (gross vs net, premium vs benefit, balance vs payment) and prefers plain-English summaries.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-to-slightly conservative in practice; okay investing when there is a clear plan, but wants a visible cash buffer and gets uneasy with sharp drawdowns.
profile.communication_style: Warm, conversational, and round-number oriented; uses ranges ("around", "give or take") and occasional filler; defers to David on technical details but interrupts when monthly outflows or complexity rise.
emotional_tendencies: stress when fixed costs feel high
reassurance from automation and simple rules
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to understand how choices affect monthly life first, then aligns once it feels manageable.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-building; trust increases with clear recaps, written follow-ups, and no jargon.
hidden_concerns: fear they are "fine on paper" but overspending
concern that tax strategies could create complexity or surprises
wants clarity on what happens if one partner can’t work or is unavailable
